name: Template Compiler with Caching
description: Implement a template compiler with AST-based rendering and caching across two modules. Module template_ast.py must define: TemplateNode abstract base class with method render(context: dict) -> str; subclasses TextNode(text), VarNode(name) renders context.get(name, ''), IfNode(var_name, body: list[TemplateNode]) renders body if context[var_name] is truthy, ForNode(var_name, iterable_name, body: list[TemplateNode]) iterates and binds var_name in inner context. Module template_engine.py must import from template_ast.py and implement Engine class with methods: compile(source: str) returns a compiled Template object exposing render(context) -> str; render(source: str, context: dict) -> str (uses internal cache so repeated identical sources reuse compiled template); cache_size() -> int. Syntax: {{ var }} for variables, {% if cond %}...{% endif %}, {% for x in items %}...{% endfor %}.
test_imports: from template_ast import TemplateNode, TextNode, VarNode, IfNode, ForNode
signature:
# template_ast.py
from abc import ABC, abstractmethod

class TemplateNode(ABC):
    @abstractmethod
    def render(self, context: dict) -> str: ...

class TextNode(TemplateNode): ...
class VarNode(TemplateNode): ...
class IfNode(TemplateNode): ...
class ForNode(TemplateNode): ...

# template_engine.py
from template_ast import TemplateNode

class Engine:
    def __init__(self): ...
    def compile(self, source: str): ...
    def render(self, source: str, context: dict) -> str: ...
    def cache_size(self) -> int: ...
tests:
def test_if_truthy():
    e = Engine()
    out = e.render('{% if show %}visible{% endif %}', {'show': True})
    assert out == 'visible'

def test_if_falsy():
    e = Engine()
    out = e.render('{% if show %}visible{% endif %}', {'show': False})
    assert out == ''

def test_for_basic():
    e = Engine()
    out = e.render('{% for x in items %}{{ x }},{% endfor %}', {'items': ['a', 'b', 'c']})
    assert out == 'a,b,c,'

def test_for_empty():
    e = Engine()
    out = e.render('{% for x in items %}X{% endfor %}', {'items': []})
    assert out == ''

def test_compile_returns_reusable():
    e = Engine()
    t = e.compile('Hi {{ name }}')
    assert t.render({'name': 'A'}) == 'Hi A'
    assert t.render({'name': 'B'}) == 'Hi B'

def test_cache_reuses():
    e = Engine()
    e.render('Hello {{ x }}', {'x': 1})
    e.render('Hello {{ x }}', {'x': 2})
    assert e.cache_size() == 1

def test_cache_distinct_sources():
    e = Engine()
    e.render('{{ a }}', {'a': 1})
    e.render('{{ b }}', {'b': 2})
    assert e.cache_size() == 2

def test_mixed():
    e = Engine()
    src = '{% if greet %}Hello {{ name }}{% endif %}'
    out = e.render(src, {'greet': True, 'name': 'World'})
    assert out == 'Hello World'

def test_for_with_var_inside():
    e = Engine()
    out = e.render('Start{% for n in nums %} {{ n }}{% endfor %} End', {'nums': [1, 2, 3]})
    assert out == 'Start 1 2 3 End'
constraints: Whitespace inside {{ }} and {% %} around tag content is trimmed
Missing variables render as empty string, not error
if/endif and for/endfor blocks must be properly paired
Engine.cache uses raw source string as cache key
compile() always produces a Template; render() may go through cache
Use the AST classes defined in template_ast.py for compiled output
